title: Coconut ice
subtitle: Pink and white no-cook sweet made with condensed milk and desiccated coconut
excerpt: Coconut ice is a no-cook confection: condensed milk and icing sugar bound with desiccated coconut, half left white and half coloured pink with a drop of red food colouring. Pressed into a tin in two layers, it sets firm overnight without any cooking. A British seaside and fairground staple.
sourceNotes: Coconut ice made with condensed milk and icing sugar is documented in British domestic confectionery guides from the twentieth century. The no-cook condensed-milk version became the standard domestic recipe from the mid-twentieth century, replacing earlier cooked-sugar versions. The two-tone pink and white presentation is standard across all British confectionery traditions for this sweet. Earlier versions using boiled sugar and desiccated coconut appear in domestic cookbooks of the late nineteenth century; the modern no-cook version is public-domain and widely reproduced in household baking guides.

content.text: Coconut ice is one of the few confections that requires no cooking equipment beyond a bowl and a tin. The challenge is getting the ratio right: too much coconut and it crumbles; too little and it stays sticky. Use the weights and compact the layers firmly.

Combine the {{condensed-milk}}, sifted {{icing-sugar}}, and {{desiccated-coconut}} in a large bowl. Mix until thoroughly combined; the mixture will be stiff and slightly sticky. Divide in half. Leave one half plain white; knead a few drops of {{food-colouring}} into the second half until the colour is even.

Line a 20 cm square tin with baking paper. Press the white mixture into the tin in an even layer, using the back of a spoon or your hands. Press firmly. Add the pink layer on top and press again until the surface is smooth. Cover and refrigerate for at least 6 to 8 hours. Read the
note: cut while still cold for clean edges.
Remove from the fridge and lift out of the tin using the baking paper. Cut into rectangles or squares with a sharp knife. Store in a sealed box at room temperature for up to 2 weeks.
